case_title: STEPHEN WEISSBERG VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL

docket_entries.description: DECLARATION OF O. ANDREW WHEATON PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE (TRANSACTION ID # 100072024) FILED BY DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.
COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR (TRANSACTION ID # 100072024) FILED BY DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.
MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN OPPOSITION TO WELLS FARGO BANK'S DEMURRER (TRANSACTION ID # 100046435) FILED BY PLAINTIFF WEISSBERG, STEPHEN
ORDER SUSTAINING DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF STEPHEN WEISSBERG'S COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF STEPHEN WEISSBERG'S COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. STATUTORY CAUSES OF ACTION MUST BE PLED WITH SPECIFICITY. (CARTER V. PRIME HEALTHCARE PARADISE VALLEY LLC (2011) 198 CAL.APP.4TH 396, 410.) PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS THAT HE SENT THREE DEMANDS FOR STATEMENT OF CONDITION TO WELLS FARGO AND DEFENDANT PETER FAZIO ON JANUARY 4, 2019, AND THAT NEITHER DEFENDANT PROVIDED A STATEMENT OF CONDITION ARE NOT PLED WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD TO WHICH ADDRESSES HE SENT THE DEMANDS, WHETHER WELLS FARGO AND MR. FAZIO RECEIVED THEM, AND WHEN ANY RESPONSE WAS PROVIDED TO PLAINTIFF. WHETHER PLAINTIFF COMPLIED WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF CIVIL CODE SECTION 2943(E)(5) THAT "THE DEMAND SHALL BE MADE TO THE BRANCH OR OFFICE SET FORTH IN THE PAYMENT BILLING NOTICE OR PAYMENT BOOK" SHOULD BE MADE APPARENT IN THE PLEADING. A VIOLATION OF CIVIL CODE SECTION 2943(E)(4) IS CONTINGENT ON A WILLFUL FAILURE TO PREPARE AND DELIVER THE STATEMENT 21 DAYS "AFTER RECEIPT OF THE WRITTEN DEMAND." THE COMPLAINT AS PLED OMITS CRUCIAL SPECIFICS ABOUT WHETHER AND WHEN DEFENDANTS RECEIVED THE DEMANDS AND IF THEY WILLFULLY DELAYED FOR 21 DAYS. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

MEMORANDUM OF P&A ISO MOTION TO RECLASSIFY CASE AS LIMITED JURISDICTION ACTION (TRANSACTION ID # 100072024) FILED BY DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-18-2019 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION TO RECLASSIFY THE CASE AS LIMITED JURISDICTION ACTION IS GRANTED. A COURT IS EMPOWERED TO RECLASSIFY AN UNLIMITED CIVIL CASE AS LIMITED IF THERE IS NO REASONABLE POSSIBILITY THAT THE COURT COULD RENDER A JUDGMENT GREATER THAN $25,000. (YTUARTE V. SUPERIOR COURT (2005) 129 CAL.APP.4TH 266, 276-277; SINGER V. SUPERIOR COURT (1999) 70 CAL.APP.4TH 1315, 1320; WALKER V. SUPERIOR COURT (1991) 53 CAL.3D 257, 269.) PLAINTIFF SEEKS STATUTORY DAMAGES OF $900 UNDER CIVIL CODE SECTION 2943. THE COMPLAINT ALSO ALLEGES THAT AS A RESULT OF DEFENDANTS' REFUSAL TO PROVIDE A STATEMENT OF CONDITION, PLAINTIFF IS UNABLE TO LEVY ON THE PROPERTY "AND HAS LOST THE ABILITY TO COLLECT INTEREST ON ITS JUDGMENT IN AN AMOUNT ACCORDING TO PROOF." (COMPL. 14, 18.) HOWEVER, THAT ALLEGATION IS INSUFFICIENT TO SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF CAN SHOW HE HAS SUSTAINED DAMAGES IN EXCESS OF THE JURISDICTIONAL LIMIT, SINCE AT MOST DEFENDANTS' FAILURE TO PROVIDE THE REQUIRED STATEMENTS MAY HAVE DELAYED PLAINTIFF'S ABILITY TO LEVY ON THE PROPERTY, AND WOULD NOT PRELUDE HIM FROM RECOVERING INTEREST ON THE UNDERLYING JUDGMENT, WHICH IS CONTINUING TO ACCRUE. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion To Reclassify The Case As Limited Jurisdiction Action
Off Calendar Jun-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s Motion To Reclassify The Case As Limited Jurisdiction Action is granted. A court is empowered to reclassify an unlimited civil case as limited if there is no reasonable possibility that the court could render a judgment greater than $25,000. (Ytuarte v. Superior Court (2005) 129 Cal.App.4th 266, 276-277; Singer v. Superior Court (1999) 70 Cal.App.4th 1315, 1320; Walker v. Superior Court (1991) 53 Cal.3d 257, 269.) Plaintiff seeks statutory damages of $900 under Civil Code section 2943. The complaint also alleges that as a result of Defendants' refusal to provide a statement of condition, Plaintiff is unable to levy on the property "and has lost the ability to collect interest on its judgment in an amount according to proof." (Compl. 14, 18.) However, that allegation is insufficient to show that Plaintiff can show he has sustained damages in excess of the jurisdictional limit, since at most Defendants' failure to provide the required statements may have delayed Plaintiff's ability to levy on the property, and would not prelude him from recovering interest on the underlying judgment, which is continuing to accrue.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Wells Fargo's demurrer to Plaintiff Stephen Weissberg's complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Statutory causes of action must be pled with specificity. (Carter v. Prime Healthcare Paradise Valley LLC (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 396, 410.) Plaintiff's allegations that he sent three demands for statement of condition to Wells Fargo and Defendant Peter Fazio on January 4, 2019, and that neither Defendant provided a statement of condition are not pled with sufficient specificity. Plaintiff has leave to amend to plead to which addresses he sent the demands, whether Wells Fargo and Mr. Fazio received them, and when any response was provided to Plaintiff. Whether Plaintiff complied with the requirements of Civil Code section 2943(e)(5) that "the demand shall be made to the branch or office set forth in the payment billing notice or payment book" should be made apparent in the pleading. A violation of Civil Code section 2943(e)(4) is contingent on a willful failure to prepare and deliver the statement 21 days "after receipt of the written demand." The complaint as pled omits crucial specifics about whether and when Defendants received the demands and if they willfully delayed for 21 days.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
